Overview

Address
7 Professional Drive, Bella Vista, AR 72714
Phone
4798553735
Certified beds
102
Avg daily residents
30 (29% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1989-06-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.70 3.39 3.07 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.29 0.59 0.33 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.08 0.75 1.23 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.33 2.04 1.51 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.37 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.85
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.34
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.24
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
100.0%
annual
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.20
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.87
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-06 23 22 2 909 2 1,364
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-02-01 10 10 0 92 1 92
Total weighted health score
1046.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (37)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
29 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
6 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. F 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-05-06 2025-10-07
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. J 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. K 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. K 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. K 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. L 2025-05-06 2025-10-07
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. L 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. F 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0844 Follow rules about disclosure of ownership requirements and tell the state agency about changes in ownership and/or administrative personnel. F 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. E 2025-05-06 2025-10-07
0848 Provide a neutral and fair arbitration process and agree to arbitrator and venue. E 2025-05-06 2025-10-07
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. F 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0946 Provide training in compliance and ethics. F 2025-05-06 2025-06-06
0729 Verify that a nurse aide has been trained; and if they haven't worked as a nurse aide for 2 years, receive retraining. F 2025-05-06 2025-10-07
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0639 Maintain 15 months of resident assessments in the resident's active clinical record. E 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. G 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. E 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. E 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-02-01 2024-02-29
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2022-10-27 2022-11-25
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. B 2022-10-27 2022-10-27
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2022-10-27 2022-11-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-10-27 2022-11-25

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
